title: The French Revolution Study Guide
tldr: The Cause: France was broke due to helping the American Revolution and suffering from bad harvests (hailstorms destroyed crops)
The Conflict: The Third Estate (98% of people) paid all taxes but had 0 power. They got mad and demanded representation
The Result: The Storming of the Bastille, the execution of Louis XVI, the Reign of Terror, and the Rise of Napoleon

sections.title: The Three Estates (Social Class System)
sections.content:
France was divided into three unequal groups called Estates:

First Estate (Clergy): Priests and the Church. They owned 10% of the land in France but paid 0% in taxes.

Second Estate (Nobility): Rich aristocrats with swords and titles. They owned 20% of the land and also paid 0% in taxes.

Third Estate (Everyone Else): Peasants, merchants, workers, and regular people. This group made up 98% of the population and paid 100% of the taxes.

sections.title: The Economic Crisis
sections.content:
King Louis XVI and Marie Antoinette spent heavily on luxury and wars:

Palace of Versailles: The royal family lived in extreme luxury while the people starved.

Seven Years War: France fought Britain and lost, draining the treasury.

American Revolution: France funded the American colonists just to spite Britain. This bankrupted France completely.

Bad Harvests: Hailstorms destroyed crops, causing bread prices to skyrocket.

sections.title: The Estates General & Tennis Court Oath
sections.content:
Desperate for money, King Louis XVI called a meeting of the Estates General - a representative assembly that hadn't met in 175 years.

Each Estate got one vote, meaning the First and Second Estates (2% of people) could always outvote the Third Estate (98%).

The Third Estate was locked out of the meeting hall. In protest, they met at a nearby indoor tennis court and swore the Tennis Court Oath - promising not to leave until France had a new Constitution.

sections.title: Storming of the Bastille
sections.content:
On July 14, 1789, angry Parisians stormed the Bastille - a prison and fortress in Paris.

They were looking for gunpowder and weapons to defend themselves against the King's troops.

The Bastille became a symbol of the uprising. The King's control over Paris was broken.

This day is now celebrated as France's national holiday (Bastille Day).

sections.title: The Reign of Terror
sections.content:
After the Revolution, Maximilien Robespierre and the Committee of Public Safety took control.

During the Reign of Terror (1793-1794), anyone suspected of being against the revolution was executed by guillotine.

An estimated 17,000-40,000 people were killed, including King Louis XVI and Marie Antoinette.

Eventually, Robespierre himself was arrested and executed, ending the Terror.

quiz.question: Which Estate paid the majority of taxes in pre-revolution France?
quiz.explanation: Only the commoners (Third Estate) paid taxes. The clergy and nobility were exempt despite owning 30% of the land combined.

quiz.question: Why was France in so much debt before the Revolution?
options: Funding the American Revolution and bad harvests
quiz.correct_answer: Funding the American Revolution and bad harvests
quiz.explanation: France spent heavily to help America fight Britain (to spite their rival), combined with crop failures that reduced tax revenue.

quiz.question: What was the Tennis Court Oath?
options: A promise to create a new French Constitution
A declaration of war against Austria
A loyalty oath to King Louis XVI
quiz.correct_answer: A promise to create a new French Constitution
quiz.explanation: After being locked out of the Estates General, the Third Estate met at a tennis court and swore not to disband until France had a constitution.

quiz.question: What event is celebrated as France's national holiday (Bastille Day)?
quiz.explanation: On July 14, 1789, revolutionaries stormed the Bastille prison. This event symbolizes the fall of royal authority.

quiz.question: Who led the Reign of Terror?
quiz.explanation: Robespierre led the Committee of Public Safety and orchestrated the Reign of Terror, executing thousands before being executed himself.

flashcards.question: What percentage of the population was the Third Estate?
Who were the king and queen during the French Revolution?
flashcards.answer: King Louis XVI and Marie Antoinette

flashcards.question: What weapon became the symbol of the Reign of Terror?
